station_count_change: JMA only; about 150-200 stations; 1993 JMA stations = 157
main_driver: legacy JMA network before rapid densification
interpretation: Sparse network. Few stations detect each event, shortest detected epicentral distance is often tens of km, observed maximum intensity is suppressed.

main_driver: mechanical intensity observation and post-Hyogo-ken Nanbu reinforcement
interpretation: First major discontinuity. Nearby detected records become much more common, raising observed maximum intensity.

station_count_change: total increases from roughly 1200 to roughly 3450; local-government stations become dominant
main_driver: municipal seismic intensity network starts being included in JMA announcements
interpretation: Main expansion phase. Station density changes enough that pre-1996 and post-1996 intensity statistics should not be compared naively.

station_count_change: total increases from roughly 3750 to over 4100
main_driver: NIED K-NET intensity information starts being included from 2004
interpretation: Network geometry is already dense, but event station records increase further; averages include more low-intensity records.

station_count_change: total stays around 4250-4400; 2019 text reports JMA 668, K-NET 792, local governments 2910, total 4370
main_driver: mature mixed network with local governments as majority
interpretation: After the rapid growth, shortest detected distance remains around several km. Maximum intensity stays high, while event mean station intensity remains low because many low-intensity stations are included.
